name: Yangshuo
intro: Yangshuo fits travelers who want the slower, more atmospheric side of the Guilin karst region, with river scenery, easy evenings, and a stronger town-and-countryside rhythm than Guilin itself.
seoTitle: Yangshuo Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Yangshuo with practical advice on whether it deserves an overnight, how it fits with Guilin, and what West Street and the Li River actually add.
tripPace: Best when travelers use Yangshuo as a selective overnight or slower scenic base, not as a place that needs constant attraction-chasing.
highlights: Adds a softer river-town and countryside texture that many first-time Guilin region trips otherwise miss.
Useful when readers want one overnight that feels scenic, walkable, and lighter than a bigger city stop.
Strongest when it is treated as an extension of the Guilin region, not a disconnected extra.

planningPriorities.title: Decide whether Yangshuo deserves a night
planningPriorities.description: A same-day pass-through and an overnight stay create very different versions of the region.

planningPriorities.title: Use town atmosphere and scenery together
planningPriorities.description: Yangshuo works best when West Street, riverside pacing, and the scenic role fit the same stop.

planningPriorities.title: Do not let the town crowd out the landscape
planningPriorities.description: The strongest first Yangshuo stop still protects the wider karst and river context.

transportNote: Yangshuo is easiest when travelers already know whether they are arriving from a Li River cruise, direct transfer from Guilin, or a wider South China route that needs one scenic overnight.
heroImageAlt: A bright aerial view of a river bend, village rooftops, and karst peaks around Yangshuo.
